reviews.review_msg: Really enjoyed attending Professor Gatev's lectures. Has some good insight and perspectives on the current climate in the global business world. Lectures aren't mandatory to succeed but definitely recommend attending. Great course to have a better idea on current events in the financial industry.

horrible. First day of class and the first thing he says is " I am not going to teach you anything, read the textbook twice before coming to class and discuss amongst your peers". Does not care about the students or their lives outside of this course. Avoid at all costs.

I like how it's discussion based and I learned a lot from talking about the news and random Finance facts. But for exams, he gives you a study guide, and then asked questions that are not even on the study guide. SO WHATS THE POINT? He is a good guy but i studied so hard following the guide and he ended up asking something else.........
People who took 316 with him probably didn't try. He's a fine professor if you're a good student.
often ignore emails, expect students to take 315 before taking 316, confusing grading system, confusing explanation , heavy dependent on textbook which is incredibly dry... should I go on?
attending lecture confuses more rather than helping...
no preparation for any lectures at all. no answer keys given to practice questions. confusing grading system..need to say more?
He makes everything very straight forward and has a good sense of humour. Wants his students to do well.

At first he would seem like a jerk but he is really a nice guy! He is helpful and wants his students to do well. He has that no student left behind policy :). He is a young fellow and explains concepts really well. Ya, the lectures were a bit boring but just read the book, pay attention in class and you would do just fine. i would do him ;-)

He comes off cold - however, he is actually a very nice guy. For instance, he brought Mars bars to the final exam for student in case they were hungry during the test. How many profs do that?? It seems as if he wants students to do well - on their own. Overall he is a good prof, and I would definitely take a class with him again.

Apathetic and unresponsive to students' concerns. Very unclear in providing instructions for assignments and exams. Lecture is a waste of time - he is always unprepared and spends a majority of the time scribbling all over his slides on his fancy tablet and talking about the iPhone and Scotiabank.
Good Prof. He wants students to understand what he is teaching.
Completely useless lecturer.  Cannot communicate, and tries to encourage interest by saying "who wants an A".  Do not take his class.  Should not be teaching
Made Finance much more interesting and easy to follow than I expected.
Easy class, though the prof doesn't put much effort in.  There's too much reliance on the text, and no answers to check with.

I normally do not complain if a prof's teaching ability is somewhat below average, but I have to say that this prof is a total trash, and I absolutely mean it. Cocky attitude. Does not reply e-mail. Shows absolutely no interest in this class. He's a disgrace to all the profs out there.
